description: Behavioral evaluation for pace-pulse (10 scenarios, ~58 assertions)

evals.name: core-pulse-cr-stagnation
evals.prompt: [AUTO] Pulse check after 5 checkpoints. Current CR has been in developing for 6 checkpoints.
evals.expected_output: Detects CR stagnation signal (>5 checkpoints threshold), outputs suggestion to split CR with actionable command. Uses natural language, prefixed with light bulb emoji.

assertions.text: Reads state.md for current work status
Detects CR stagnation signal (checkpoint count > 5)
Output prefixed with light-bulb suggestion format
Suggests splitting CR into smaller changes
Output is 1-2 lines, natural language
Writes timestamp to .devpace/.pulse-last-run

evals.name: core-pulse-no-signal-silence
evals.prompt: [AUTO] Pulse check after 5 checkpoints. All signals within normal thresholds.
evals.expected_output: No signals triggered. Complete silence - no output at all.

assertions.text: Scans all signal sources (state.md, current.md, backlog/, metrics/)
No signal exceeds threshold
Produces no output (complete silence)
Writes timestamp to .devpace/.pulse-last-run

evals.name: core-pulse-positive-feedback
evals.prompt: [AUTO] Pulse check after CR merged. No anomaly signals detected.
evals.expected_output: No anomaly signals. Outputs positive feedback with checkmark prefix (max 1 per session).

assertions.text: Detects CR merged event with no anomaly signals
Outputs checkmark-prefixed positive feedback
Positive feedback does not count toward session quota
Maximum 1 positive feedback per session

evals.name: session-start-layered-summary
evals.prompt: [AUTO] Session start rhythm check. 2 in_review CRs, iteration at 85% completion, 1 high risk open.
evals.expected_output: Loads session-start + snooze procedures. Outputs layered summary: highest priority signal as full suggestion, remaining as condensed list. Total <=3 lines.

assertions.text: Loads pulse-procedures-session-start.md and pulse-procedures-snooze.md
Highest priority signal (in_review CRs) shown as full suggestion
Lower priority signals condensed into compact list
Total output at most 3 lines
Each suggestion includes actionable command reference
Uses natural language, no internal IDs or state machine terms

evals.name: session-end-rhythm-summary
evals.prompt: [AUTO] Session end. 2 CRs completed (1 merged, 1 in_review), iteration progress 65% -> 73%.
evals.expected_output: Loads session-end procedures. Outputs 1-2 line session summary with chart prefix showing CR completions and iteration progress delta.

assertions.text: Loads pulse-procedures-session-end.md
Output prefixed with chart-bar summary format
Reports CR completion count with status breakdown
Reports iteration progress delta
Does not count toward session quota

evals.name: signal-grouping-composite-diagnosis
evals.prompt: [AUTO] Pulse check. CR stuck for 7 checkpoints + 3 CRs merged this session + 4 Gate 1 passes this session.
evals.expected_output: Detects 3 signals in efficiency group. Outputs composite diagnosis instead of 3 individual suggestions. Counts as 1 quota item.

assertions.text: Detects CR stagnation, high productivity, and context density signals simultaneously
Recognizes all 3 belong to efficiency signal group
Outputs composite diagnosis template instead of individual suggestions
Composite diagnosis counts as 1 quota item
Includes actionable commands (compact and CR split suggestion)

evals.name: severity-escalation
evals.prompt: [AUTO] Pulse check. Review backlog was 3 (triggered earlier), now at 6 in_review CRs.
evals.expected_output: Same signal but severity doubled (3 to 6). Outputs escalated warning with warning prefix instead of suggestion prefix. Counts toward quota.

assertions.text: Detects review backlog signal was previously triggered at value 3
Current value (6) >= 2x previous value (3), triggers escalation
Output uses escalation format (warning prefix) instead of suggestion format
Output includes updated count in suggestion
Escalation counts toward session quota

evals.name: snooze-wakeup-detection
evals.prompt: [AUTO] CR merged for PF-001. Snoozed CR-005 has trigger condition 'PF-001 complete'.
evals.expected_output: Loads snooze procedures. Detects trigger condition met for snoozed CR. Outputs non-blocking reminder, appends reminded marker to CR record.

assertions.text: Loads pulse-procedures-snooze.md
Scans backlog/ for snooze records matching merged PF
Detects trigger condition is satisfied
Outputs non-blocking reminder with snooze context (reason + CR + PF)
Appends reminded marker to snooze record to prevent duplicate reminders
Snooze reminder does not count toward session quota

evals.name: gc-scan-stale-docs
evals.prompt: [AUTO] Session start. .devpace/project.md last modified 45 days ago. All other signals normal.
evals.expected_output: GC scan detects stale documentation (>30 days). Outputs cleanup suggestion with broom prefix. Does not interrupt higher priority signals.

assertions.text: Loads pulse-procedures-gc.md during session start
Detects document staleness (>30 days threshold)
Output uses broom prefix for GC suggestions
GC suggestion uses independent quota (max 1, does not count toward core pulse quota)
GC suggestion is lowest priority (priority 12)

evals.name: gc-scan-decay-detection
evals.prompt: [AUTO] Session start. insights.md has 3 Active entries with last_referenced dates over 200 days ago.
evals.expected_output: GC scan detects confidence decay needed. Outputs ACTION delegating to /pace-learn prune --decay-only. Does not directly modify insights.md.

assertions.text: Loads pulse-procedures-gc.md during session start
Reads insights.md and detects Active entries with last_referenced > 180 days
Output includes ACTION: /pace-learn prune --decay-only
Does not directly modify insights.md (IA-6: pace-learn is sole writer)
Output uses broom prefix
